history.content:
<thought>
In my current view, the goal `G` is at row 05 column F, which is to my right and slightly ahead. The cell directly in front of me (row 06 column D) is `.` (safe), but moving forward would not get closer to the goal as directly as turning right first. The cell to my right (row 07 column E) is also `.` (safe), so I will rotate clockwise to face that direction and approach the goal while avoiding lava `L` cells at row 06 columns E and F.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can see the goal `G` at row 05 column B, which is to my left. I am currently facing up (the `^` at row 07 column D corresponds to my position; the cell in front is the one directly above it). The cells to the left include the goal direction, while moving forward would lead toward walls `#`. Since the cell to my left is `.`/`G` (not lava), I should turn left to align with the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal `G` is visible at row 05 column F. From my current position at row 07 column D, the cell directly in front (toward row 06 column D) is `.` (safe). Moving forward will bring me closer to the goal while avoiding the lava `L` cells at row 06 columns E and F.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am now at row 06 column D (marked `^`). The goal `G` is at row 06 column F, two cells to the right. The immediate right cell (row 06 column E) is `.` so it’s safe; moving forward is blocked by `#` ahead. I should turn right to face row 06 column E and then move.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
From my position at row 07 column D (`^`), the goal `G` is at row 05 column C and is no longer directly in view, but I can see it two cells ahead and left relative to my current orientation. The cell directly in front is `.` (row 06 column D is `L`), so moving forward would hit lava. I should turn left to face toward the safer path near the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing upward. In front (row 06 column D) is `.` which is safe. Turning isn’t needed; I will move forward toward the goal while avoiding lava cells.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing up. Ahead is row 06 column D which is `?` (unknown), so I can’t be sure it’s safe. To avoid stepping into potential lava, I’ll instead turn right to look for a safe path that reaches the visible goal at row 07 column F.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing up. The cell in front (row 06 column D) is `.` and safe. The goal is at row 05 column D, which is two steps ahead, so moving forward is the right way to get there while avoiding the visible lava at row 05 column E.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m now at row 07 column D facing up. The goal is at row 06 column D, which is `G`, and the cell directly ahead is that goal. Moving forward will reach it, and there is no lava on the destination cell.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
